name: Professor Pritam Singh
title: Emeritus Professor of Economics
bio: Professor Pritam Singh is Emeritus Professor of Economics at Oxford Brookes Business School (awarded Emeritus status 2018). His expertise spans development economics, environmental economics, comparative economic systems, and the political economy of human rights and nationalism, particularly related to India and Punjab. He leads the research cluster on Environmental and Development Economics at Oxford Brookes University. His work focuses on the sustainability implications of the spatial shift in global capitalism and Indian capitalism with emphasis on decentralisation and human rights.

education.DPhil: University of Oxford (Oriel College), awarded Edward Boyle/Charles Wallace Scholarship
education.MPhil: Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi, India (awarded with distinction, Junior Research Fellowship by the University Grants Commission of India)
education.MA_BA: Panjab University, Chandigarh, India (BA Hons School in Economics and MA Hons School in Economics)

prior_positions: Lecturer in Economics, University of Delhi
Reader in Economics, Panjab University, Chandigarh
Research Associate, Department of International Development (Queen Elizabeth House), University of Oxford (from 2007)
Visiting Professor, Jawaharlal Nehru University, Delhi (2009)
Visiting Professor, Moscow State University, Moscow (2013)
Visiting appointments at Federal University of Uberlandia, Brazil
research_interests: Political Economy of Nationalism and Human Rights
administrative_roles: Leads research cluster on Environmental and Development Economics at Oxford Brookes
Managing Committee member, Association for Heterodox Economics (UK)
Economic Advisor to British House of Commons All-Party Parliamentary Committee for Punjabis in Britain
Editorial board member, Brookes eJournal of Teaching and Learning
Editorial board member, International Journal of Pluralism and Economics Education
External advisor on validation panels for economics and business degrees at Coventry University and City University of Birmingham
supervision: Actively involved in doctoral student supervision as Director of Studies. Has supervised three PhDs to successful completion (Michael Ochurub, 2001; V.K. Mensah, 2008; Lok Nath Bhusal, 2012) and is currently supervising seven PhD students as Director of Studies, including one from Burgundy Business School, Dijon and one by publication route. Has externally examined PhDs for universities in the UK, Ireland, India and Pakistan. Also on the supervisory/examination committee of a PhD student at the State University of New Jersey, Rutgers.
